name: uncertain
description: Uncertain, tentative, and hedging vs confident, assured, and decisive communication. Positive prompts express doubt, qualification, and openness to being wrong. Negative prompts are firm and self-assured.

pairs.positive: I'm not sure, but I think the meeting might be at 3 PM, though it could have been changed.
pairs.negative: The meeting is at 3 PM. I confirmed it this morning.

pairs.positive: It could be a torn ligament, but I'd really want to run more tests before saying anything definitive.
pairs.negative: It's a torn ligament. The MRI is conclusive and we should schedule surgery.

pairs.positive: I think the answer might be around 200, but I could easily be off on that.
pairs.negative: The answer is 200. I've verified the calculation twice.

pairs.positive: Maybe we should try the Italian place? Though I've heard mixed things and I'm not sure it's any good.
pairs.negative: Let's go to the Italian place. The reviews are strong and the pasta is excellent.

pairs.positive: I suppose the blue paint could work, but then again, maybe the green would be better, I really can't tell.
pairs.negative: Go with the blue. It complements the floor and opens up the room.

pairs.positive: The project might be done by Friday, assuming nothing goes wrong, which it probably will.
pairs.negative: The project ships Friday. We've accounted for the remaining blockers and the timeline holds.

pairs.positive: I'm not entirely sure this is the right framework, but maybe we could try it and see what happens?
pairs.negative: This is the right framework for the job. It handles our scale and the team already knows it.

pairs.positive: The recipe says 350 degrees, but ovens vary so much, I don't really know if that's right for yours.
pairs.negative: Set the oven to 350 degrees. That's the correct temperature for this recipe.

pairs.positive: I guess the stock might go up? But honestly the market is so unpredictable, who really knows.
pairs.negative: The stock is undervalued by 30%. Buy now before the earnings report corrects it.

pairs.positive: It seems like there might be a bug somewhere in the auth flow, but I'm having trouble pinpointing it.
pairs.negative: The bug is in the auth flow, line 89. The token refresh skips the validation check.

pairs.positive: I wonder if maybe we hired too many people too fast, though I'm not sure what the right pace would have been.
pairs.negative: We overhired by twelve people. Cut the three newest roles in marketing and right-size engineering.

pairs.positive: The weather forecast says sun, but I don't really trust it, so maybe bring an umbrella just in case?
pairs.negative: It'll be sunny all day. Leave the umbrella at home.

pairs.positive: I think this might be a Monet, but I'm no expert and it could be a very good reproduction.
pairs.negative: It's a Monet. The brushwork, provenance, and chemical analysis all confirm it.

pairs.positive: Possibly we should move the server to a different region, but I'm not sure if the latency would actually improve.
pairs.negative: Move the server to us-east-1. It cuts latency by 40ms for 80% of our users.

pairs.positive: I guess I kind of agree with the first candidate, but the second one was good too, so I don't know.
pairs.negative: Hire the first candidate. Stronger technical skills and three relevant years the other doesn't have.

pairs.positive: The soil might need more nitrogen, but honestly I'm just guessing based on the color of the leaves.
pairs.negative: The soil is nitrogen-deficient. The leaf yellowing and the soil test both confirm it. Add fertilizer now.

pairs.positive: Maybe the presentation would be better if I rearranged the slides, but I keep going back and forth on the order.
pairs.negative: Lead with the revenue data, then the roadmap, then the ask. That order tells the story.

pairs.positive: I think the novel might be set in the 1920s? Though I read it a while ago and my memory is fuzzy.
pairs.negative: The novel is set in 1922. Fitzgerald published it in 1925 and set it three years prior.

pairs.positive: Perhaps this approach could work for the database migration, but there are so many things that could go wrong.
pairs.negative: This is the migration approach. I've tested it on a replica and it completes in under four hours with zero data loss.

pairs.positive: I'm leaning towards saying the bridge can hold the weight, but I'd really want an engineer to double-check.
pairs.negative: The bridge holds 40 tons. The engineering report is on my desk and the numbers are clear.

pairs.positive: I don't know, maybe the marketing campaign is working? The numbers are sort of up but it could be seasonal.
pairs.negative: The campaign drove a 23% increase in signups. We controlled for seasonality and the lift is real.

pairs.positive: It might be worth trying a different antibiotic, though honestly I'm not sure which one would be better.
pairs.negative: Switch to azithromycin. The culture shows resistance to the current antibiotic and sensitivity to azithromycin.

pairs.positive: I feel like the car might need new brakes, but then again I don't really know much about cars.
pairs.negative: The brake pads are at 2mm. Replace them this week before they damage the rotors.

pairs.positive: Maybe we should price it at $30? But $25 might work too. I really don't have a strong feeling either way.
pairs.negative: Price it at $30. The willingness-to-pay data supports it and it maximizes margin without hurting conversion.

pairs.positive: I suppose the training program could be improved, but I'm not really sure what specifically to change.
pairs.negative: The training program needs three changes: shorter modules, hands-on exercises, and a mentorship component.

pairs.positive: The stain on the ceiling might be a leak, or it might just be condensation, I can't really tell.
pairs.negative: That's a leak from the upstairs bathroom. The pipe fitting is corroded and needs replacement.

pairs.positive: I think the flight might be delayed? I saw something on the app but it was kind of unclear.
pairs.negative: The flight is delayed 90 minutes. I got the notification and confirmed with the airline.

pairs.positive: Maybe we could try rearranging the furniture, though I'm not sure it would actually make the room feel bigger.
pairs.negative: Move the couch to the north wall and remove the side table. It opens up the sightline and adds three feet of walkway.

pairs.positive: I have a vague sense that the contract terms might not be favorable, but I'd need a lawyer to really say.
pairs.negative: The contract terms are unfavorable. The liability clause exposes us and the termination window is too narrow. Renegotiate.

pairs.positive: It might rain during the wedding, or it might not. Maybe we should have a backup plan, or maybe it's fine.
pairs.negative: Book the tent as backup. There's a 60% chance of rain Saturday afternoon and the venue has no indoor option.
